rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core migration logic and both independently surface the latent gap (the unswept diffusion-transformer caller of get_cache_scale), satisfying gap_hit. Y is more rigorous: it cross-references actual upstream vLLM source (AutoWeightsLoader, weight_utils.py, fp8.py) to substantiate its correctness claims rather than asserting them, and its top finding — that the delegated-loader test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader and thus never proves the outer mapping actually happens — closely mirrors lishunyang12's real non-blocking concern about unverified real-checkpoint behavior. X's findings are valid and well-cited but skew toward low-value nits (docstring wording, assert ordering) and one speculative 'please confirm' ask that it doesn't verify itself. X does explicitly namecheck #4808, giving it a slight edge on that one GT point, but overall Y's precision and depth are stronger.
